chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Justice is Obligatory Towards the Camel, Avoiding Beating it, and Refraining From Oppressing it

text: : مَرَّ قِطَارٌ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَرَأَى زَامِلَةً قَدْ مَالَتْ فَقَالَ «يَا غُلاَمُ اِعْدِلْ عَلَى هَذَا اَلْحَمْلِ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ اَلْعَدْلَ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2492 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Hammad al-Lahham, who said:
A caravan passed by Abu Abdullah (as), and he noticed a pack camel that had leaned to one side under its load.
Imam (as) said: "O boy, adjust the load on this animal, for Allah (swt), the Exalted, loves justice."
